title: resonbank2~

messages.description: real part of the resonator bank

flags.name: -partial <list>
flags.description: list of frequencies for all filters

flags.name: -decay <list>
flags.description: list of decay times for all filters

flags.name: -amp <list>
flags.description: list of amplitudes for all filters

flags.name: -ramp <list>
flags.description: list of ramp times for all filters

flags.name: -rampall <float>
flags.description: sets ramp time for all filters

methods.description: sets list of partials for the resonators's frequencies
list of decay times for all filters
list of amplitudes for all filters
list of ramp times for all filters
sets ramp time for all filters

body: [resonbank2~] is a bank of resonators made of [resonator2~] objects. The design and structure here is different than [resonator2~] in order to make it better suited for sound synthesis. It has the same design as [resonbank~] and has a list of partials for each resonator and a frequency multiplier in the left inlet, kinda like [oscbank~]. The mid inlet the trigger (or excitation) signal (kinda like [pluck~]) - this must be an impulse since noise sources and noise bursts generate very loud outputs! The rightmost inlet is a time multiplier for the decay times. The number of filters is set via the parameter list size (such as the partial list). There's also support for multichannel output.
